title: Truva – From Fragmented CRM Data to Unified Customer Engagement through Zoho–Netcore Integration
subtitle: Building a scalable, event-driven data pipeline to unify CRM and marketing automation systems
role: Zoho CRM & Netcore Cloud Unified Data Synchronization

isBrandedMetric.title: Total Events Processed
isBrandedMetric.description: Club Mahindra set out to scale its Customer Lifecycle Marketing

overview: Truva, a growing real-estate platform, manages an extensive ecosystem of buyers, sellers, and channel partners. Each stakeholder interacts with the business through different touchpoints- leads, property listings, and partner onboarding workflows — creating rich yet fragmented data streams across multiple Zoho CRM modules, including Leads, Sellers, Properties, and Channel Partners.

These isolated modules posed a significant challenge: data lived in silos within Zoho CRM, making it difficult to activate, synchronize, or personalize engagement across the three key Netcore Cloud panels — Buyer, Seller, and Channel Partner.

Attributics partnered with Truva to design and implement a robust data synchronization and automation framework, enabling real-time, one-way communication between Zoho CRM and Netcore Cloud using a fully event-driven architecture on AWS. This end-to-end integration automated lead syncing, event triggers, and cross-platform updates, empowering Truva's marketing and CRM teams to act on live customer signals, streamline workflows, and improve overall engagement efficiency.

challenge.subtitle: Multi-Panel Synchronization Complexity
challenge.description: Each Netcore Cloud panel (Buyer, Seller, and Channel Partner) had unique schema and data mapping needs, demanding a flexible and automated synchronization logic.

challenge.subtitle: Workflow & Event Dependencies
challenge.description: Multiple events in Zoho CRM — such as property updates and acquisition status changes — required corresponding event triggers in Netcore Cloud, which had to be automated and reliable.

challenge.subtitle: Scalability & Reliability
challenge.description: The integration needed to handle increasing lead volumes while maintaining high uptime, ensuring no data duplication or failure under heavy API usage.

solution.subtitle: Retention
solution.description: PCOS, Jeehv, TULA, SOMHA, Haridrakhand (Hindi), Medohar, Iron Folic Acid, Calcium-VitaminD3, Menopause

solution.subtitle: Win-back
solution.description: PCOS win-back at 30–45, 46–60, 61–75, and 76–90 day intervals (English + Hindi)

solution.subtitle: Transactional
solution.description: Order Confirmation SMS, Order Placed WhatsApp, Clinic Post-Consultation Drip, Doctor Adherence Call

solution.subtitle: NPS & Surveys
solution.description: Product-specific NPS nudges and Repeat Purchase Survey across 8 SKUs

solution.subtitle: Acquisition
solution.description: Abandon Cart, COS Communication, Jeehv JoinCOS, Cross-sell PCOS–Medohar

process.title: Architecture & Data Flow Design
process.description: Designed a scalable, event-driven architecture to enable real-time, one-way synchronization between Zoho CRM and Netcore Cloud. Defined end-to-end data flow from Zoho workflows → Deluge functions → AWS API Gateway → SQS → Lambda → Netcore panels.

process.title: Data Orchestration & Queuing
process.description: Implemented AWS API Gateway to capture Zoho CRM webhooks and route data to SQS queues. Configured asynchronous message handling with retries, Dead Letter Queues (DLQ), and 14-day retention to ensure reliability under high traffic.

process.title: Data Transformation & Validation
process.description: Built AWS Lambda (Node.js) functions to transform Zoho CRM data into Netcore-compatible schema. Supported Buyer, Seller, and Channel Partner panels using dynamic userType mapping and enforced strict validation using Zod.

process.title: Workflow Automation in Zoho CRM
process.description: Developed multiple Deluge functions attached to Zoho workflows to trigger real-time data syncs on key events such as property updates, acquisition stages, and lead qualification.

process.title: Monitoring, Backup & Error Handling
process.description: Implemented Amazon S3 for JSON payload backups, CloudWatch for monitoring API and Lambda performance, and Sentry for real-time error tracking and debugging.

results.metric: All Zoho CRM modules (Leads, Sellers, Properties, Channel Partners) integrated with Netcore panels
Fully automated data synchronization framework eliminating manual processes
Reliable system with SQS retries and DLQ ensuring no data loss during failures
Real-time campaign activation based on CRM updates across Buyer, Seller, and Partner panels
AWS-based serverless pipeline scaling automatically with increasing lead volume

techStack.title: CRM Platform
techStack.description: Zoho CRM (Leads, Sellers, Properties, Channel Partners modules)

techStack.title: Marketing Automation
techStack.description: Netcore Cloud (Buyer, Seller, Channel Partner panels)

techStack.title: Message Queue
techStack.description: Amazon SQS (with retries, DLQ, retention policies)

conclusion: The Zoho CRM–Netcore integration project represents a major step in Truva's data modernization journey. By partnering with Attributics, Truva successfully bridged the gap between Zoho CRM and Netcore Cloud, establishing a unified, event-driven data pipeline that drives customer engagement and operational excellence.

This transformation empowered Truva's teams to move beyond fragmented CRM processes toward a connected, automated, and intelligent engagement ecosystem — setting a strong foundation for future analytics, personalization, and growth initiatives.
